params.text:
Progress Note - Day 2 Subjective: Patient reports improved breathing with current inhaler regimen. Denies chest pain at present. Tolerating room air without dyspnea. Objective: VS: BP 128/78, HR 78, SpO2 96% RA. Labs: Creatinine 1.1, troponin normal. Patient appears comfortable. Assessment:
1. Hypertension (I10) - well controlled on current regimen
2. Chest pain (R07.9) - resolved, troponin negative
3. Long-term drug therapy (Z79.899) - continuing Plan:
1. Inhaler regimen optimization - continue current bronchodilator therapy
2. Oxygen reassessment - SpO2 96% on room air, no supplemental O2 required
3. Follow-up coordination - outpatient pulmonary follow-up recommended
4. Continue monitoring

params.message: Please coordinate outpatient pulmonary follow-up for patient-v2-coding_billing-diri-008. Patient has optimized inhaler regimen and is stable on room air (SpO2 96%). Schedule follow-up within 1-2 weeks post-discharge for inhaler technique review and spirometry if needed.

params.plan_text: Discharge Plan for patient-v2-coding_billing-diri-008: 1. Inhaler Regimen: Continue current bronchodilator therapy as prescribed. Patient demonstrates proper inhaler technique. 2. Oxygen: No supplemental oxygen required. SpO2 96% on room air. Reassessed and stable. 3. Follow-up: - Pulmonary clinic appointment within 1-2 weeks (care team notified) - Primary care follow-up within 2 weeks for hypertension management - Return if shortness of breath worsens or chest pain recurs 4. Medications: Continue antihypertensive therapy and current inhaler regimen 5. Activity: No restrictions. Gradual return to normal activities. Evidence: SpO2 96% RA, BP 128/78 controlled, troponin negative, chest pain resolved.
